executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

National Medicaid enrollment has stabilized at 75.3 million beneficiaries as of January 2026, though most states continue experiencing enrollment declines following the end of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. The largest states show varied trajectories, with Michigan posting the steepest decline at -7.6% year-over-year, while North Carolina bucked the trend with modest growth of +0.5%. Texas, with 4.0 million enrollees, experienced a moderate -3.1% decline, comparable to Pennsylvania's reduction and better than California's -6.2% drop. National spending reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, translating to $2,869 per enrollee, with the federal government covering approximately 66% of total costs. Per-enrollee spending varies dramatically across states, ranging from Florida's efficient $2,031 per beneficiary to Pennsylvania's $4,377, while Texas maintains a moderate $2,988 per enrollee—slightly above the national average but below peer states like Ohio ($3,561) and New York ($3,431).

Quality performance presents concerning disparities, with the national average score of 65.6 out of 100 masking significant state-level variations. High-performing states like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80) substantially outpace struggling programs in the South, where Mississippi scored just 48. Texas ranks 30th nationally with a score of 62, placing it in the moderate performance tier but below the national average and suggesting room for improvement relative to leading states. The state's managed care penetration of 93.3% exceeds the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong infrastructure for coordinated care delivery. Recent federal regulatory activity includes new provider enrollment moratoria and updated payment parameters for 2027, while ongoing procurement opportunities in Texas and heightened OIG oversight activities signal continued federal attention to program integrity and performance across all state programs.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, representing a significant but measured decline of 3.1% year-over-year as the state continues post-pandemic enrollment unwinding. This decline rate falls between the national pattern, aligning closely with Pennsylvania's 3.1% decrease while outperforming larger states like California (-6.2%) and Illinois (-6.2%). Among non-expansion southern peers, Texas's enrollment contraction is more moderate than Georgia (-3.6%) but steeper than North Carolina (+0.5%), which has benefited from Medicaid expansion. The state's managed care penetration rate of 93.3% significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, demonstrating Texas's commitment to coordinated care delivery models with nearly 3.9 million enrollees in managed care arrangements.

Texas faces notable challenges in program efficiency and quality outcomes that warrant close monitoring. At $2,988 per enrollee, Texas spends 4.2% above the national average of $2,869, yet delivers quality scores of just 62/100—ranking 30th nationally and falling into the "moderate" performance tier. This creates an unfavorable cost-quality dynamic compared to expansion states like North Carolina, which achieves better outcomes ($2,616 per enrollee, though quality scores weren't specified) and even non-expansion Florida ($2,031 per enrollee). However, Texas maintains operational strengths through its robust managed care infrastructure and active procurement activities, as evidenced by ongoing HHSC contracting opportunities. Key areas to watch include whether the state's higher per-enrollee spending translates into quality improvements and how effectively Texas navigates federal regulatory changes around state-directed payments and provider enrollment moratoria that could impact its large managed care ecosystem.

alerts.title: Michigan Medicaid enrollment drops 7.6% year-over-year
alerts.description: Michigan experienced a significant 7.6% decline in Medicaid enrollment, well above the 10% threshold for concern and indicating potential access issues or eligibility changes.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania per-enrollee spending 53% above national average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,869, suggesting potential cost control issues or unique population needs.

alerts.title: Florida spending substantially below national average
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,031 is 29% below the national average, potentially indicating inadequate coverage or access limitations despite large enrollment.

alerts.title: Five states show critically low quality scores below 55
alerts.description: Mississippi, Oklahoma, Alabama, South Carolina, and Arkansas have quality scores below 55, indicating significant care quality concerns requiring immediate attention.

alerts.title: Temporary enrollment moratoriums announced nationwide
alerts.description: Recent Federal Register announcements indicate temporary moratoriums on enrollment for Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP programs that may impact future enrollment trends.

executiveInsights.title: TX enrollment declining at 3.1% with $12B total expenditures at risk
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% YoY to 4.03M enrollees while maintaining $12.05B in total expenditures. This enrollment decline could signal redetermination impacts or eligibility tightening affecting contract volumes.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment declines directly impact Accenture's contract value and operational scope, requiring immediate discussion with HHSC about adjusting service levels and resource allocation.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Schedule immediate discussion with HHSC leadership about enrollment trends impact on contract scope

executiveInsights.title: TX quality score at 62/100 ranks #30, below national average of 65.6
executiveInsights.summary: Texas quality performance at 62/100 falls significantly below the national average and ranks in the bottom half of states. This moderate tier performance may trigger federal scrutiny or corrective action requirements.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality scores can lead to CMS sanctions, reduced federal funding, or mandatory improvement plans that would directly impact Accenture's deliverables and success metrics.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement initiatives to HHSC and review current performance metrics

executiveInsights.title: TX managed care penetration at 93.3% exceeds national 83.2% average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration covering 3.86M enrollees, significantly above the 83.2% national average. This high penetration indicates mature managed care operations but may limit further expansion opportunities.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration suggests Texas operations are heavily dependent on managed care systems, requiring Accenture to focus on optimization rather than expansion strategies.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review managed care optimization opportunities rather than expansion strategies

executiveInsights.title: Federal moratorium announcements may impact TX provider enrollment
executiveInsights.summary: Multiple Federal Register notices announce nationwide temporary moratoriums on Medicare and Medicaid provider enrollment. These regulatory changes could affect Texas provider networks and enrollment processes.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider enrollment moratoriums could disrupt network adequacy requirements and create operational challenges for Accenture's provider relations and enrollment support functions.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review Federal Register moratorium details and assess impact on TX provider operations

executiveInsights.title: TX per-enrollee costs at $2,988 vs national average $2,869
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spending $2,988 per enrollee is slightly above the national average of $2,869, ranking 4th in total expenditures at $12.05B. This indicates relatively controlled costs despite high enrollment volumes.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Above-average per-enrollee costs suggest potential for cost management initiatives that Accenture could propose to HHSC, creating opportunities for additional value-based contracts.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Develop cost management proposals highlighting TX performance vs national benchmarks

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration creates high-stakes procurement opportunities as contracts come up for renewal.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal actively and prepare comprehensive managed care transformation proposals.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality measure gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, creating improvement mandate and consulting opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop targeted quality improvement roadmap focusing on Texas's specific performance gaps.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline impact
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's -3.1% YoY enrollment decline may trigger budget pressures and operational restructuring needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position cost optimization and efficiency services to help HHSC manage declining enrollment impacts.

riskOpportunity.label: State directed payments
riskOpportunity.description: New federal regulations on state directed payments create compliance complexity requiring specialized expertise.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Build regulatory compliance advisory capability for managed care state directed payment requirements.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider enrollment moratorium
riskOpportunity.description: Federal temporary moratorium on provider enrollment creates network adequacy challenges for managed care plans.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop provider network management solutions to help plans maintain adequacy during enrollment restrictions.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG oversight intensification
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG Medicaid portfolio oversight increases compliance scrutiny and audit risk for state programs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen compliance monitoring capabilities and prepare rapid response protocols for potential OIG reviews.

riskOpportunity.label: MMIS modernization cycle
riskOpportunity.description: Texas HHSC procurement opportunities suggest upcoming system modernization creating technology transformation needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position integrated MMIS modernization services combining technology and operational transformation expertise.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-enrollee cost efficiency
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's $2,988 per-enrollee cost above Florida ($2,031) but below national average creates optimization pressure.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Benchmark Texas operations against efficient states and propose targeted cost reduction initiatives.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 4,032,864 Medicaid enrollees as of January 2026, ranking 3rd nationally.

pulseMetrics.label: 12-Mo Enrollment Delta
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas enrollment declined 3.1% year-over-year, performing better than the national average decline.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $2,988 per Medicaid enrollee annually, slightly above the national average of $2,869.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 enrollees in managed care plans.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on Medicaid quality measures, ranking 30th nationally in moderate tier.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two active Texas procurement portals are being monitored for Medicaid-related opportunities.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Ten federal regulatory and OIG items are currently active that may impact Texas Medicaid operations.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific upcoming milestones identified in the available data.
